task.title: Install Portfolio Manager on macOS (Dock Shortcut)
task.shortdesc: Install Portfolio Manager as a macOS application bundle and add it to the Dock for one-click daily access.

ul.li: Python 3.11 or later from python.org. The python.org installer includes Tkinter. Python installed via Homebrew may not include Tkinter. Use the python.org installer to avoid launch failures.
An internet connection for the initial clone

step.cmd: Open Terminal and clone the Portfolio Manager repository:
Run the macOS shortcut creation script:

info.p: The script creates a Portfolio Manager.app bundle in ~/Applications/ and sets up a Python virtual environment in .venv/ inside the repository.

step.cmd: Drag Portfolio Manager.app to the right side of the Dock.
Click the Dock icon to launch Portfolio Manager for the first time.
step.stepresult: Portfolio Manager opens, creates the database and configuration file at ~/.portfolio_manager/, and displays the Dashboard.

postreq.p: To update the application, run git pull origin main in the repository directory. The next time you launch from the Dock, the updated code runs automatically. No reinstallation is required.
